name: Héctor Lavoe
abstract: Héctor Juan Pérez Martínez  (September 30, 1946 – June 29, 1993), better known as Héctor Lavoe, was a Puerto Rican salsa singer. Widely regarded as one of salsa's most important and influential vocalists, Lavoe played a pivotal role in popularizing the genre throughout the 1960s, '70s, and '80s.

concerts.label: Newport Jazz Festival: Schlitz Salute to Latin Roots
Newport Jazz Festival: Schlitz Salute to Latin Roots

about_quotes.speaker: Eddie Palmieri
about_quotes.evidence: Like Hector Lavoe from Willie Colon; Pete El Conde from Pacheco; Ismael Miranda from Larry Harlow... Every singer that went on their own, they did...you know, that kind of thing.
about_quotes.doc_title: Eddie Palmieri — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Tom Malone
about_quotes.evidence: I played on all the salsa recordings in New York too. Fania Records, 1974, ’75, ’76. Willie Colon, Hector Lavoe, Ruben Blades... All those guys.
about_quotes.doc_title: Tom Malone interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, May 3, 2023
